rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BLANDINA D. PADLAN REVOCABLE TRUST OF 5/16/00 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions In The Form Of Monetary Penalties Under Code Civ Proc 128.7 Against Harald Westendorf And For An Award Of Expenses And Attorneys Fees Against Alexis D . Padlan And In Favor Of The Blandina D. Padlan Trust
rulings.Rulings: THIS TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN POSTED AFTER 3:00 P.M. ON JULY 16, 2019. Defendant and Trustee Zenaida Graves' Motion for Monetary Sanctions Under CCP § 128.7 Against Attorney Harald Westendorf and Motion for Expenses and Fees Against Alexis Padlan and in Favor of the Blandina D. Padlan Trust is DENIED. "A motion for sanctions under [CCP 128.7] shall be made separately from other motions or requests. . . . Notice of motion shall be served as provided in [CCP] Section 1010, but shall not be filed with or presented to the court unless, within 21 days after service of the motion, or any other period as the court may prescribe, the challenged paper, claim, defense, contention, allegation, or denial is not withdrawn or appropriately corrected." (CCP, § 128.7, subd. (c)(1).) The record contains no evidence that Moving Party served the notice of motion consistent with subdivision (c)(1)'s "safe harbor" provision. Further, "[t]he law is well settled that the [CCP § 128.7] safe harbor provisions preclude a party from seeking monetary sanctions for an offending pleading following a dispositive ruling by the trial court on the pleading." (Malovec v. Hamrell (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 434, 441, citing Goodstone v. Southwest Airlines Co. (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 406; Cromwell v. Cummings (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th Supp. 10; Barber v. Miller (9th Cir.1998) 146 F.3d 707; Ridder v. City of Springfield (1997) 109 F.3d 288.) Here, the Court has already made a final determination and ruling on the underlying petition. Additionally, to the extent the motion is based on discovery activities, it is denied as improper. (See CCP § 128.7, subd. (g) ["This section shall not apply to disclosures and discovery requests, responses, objections, and motions."].) Independently, the motion is denied as without merit. Petitioner Alexis Padlan's request for fees and costs is DENIED. Considered under CCP § 128.7, subdivision (c)(1), the request for "$2,000 for having to defend the June 5, 2019 motion to set aside the Court's orders and the present motion" is overbroad to the extent it includes costs and fees for the underlying motion and not merited for the 1.5 page "response" on this motion and, in any event, unsupported by evidence. Considered under CCP § 128.7, subdivision (h), the request is denied as improper because such a request must be made in a properly served and filed "motion for sanctions." (See CCP § 128.7, subd. (h).) Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, July 16, 2019
